results.title: Death row inmate who’s had three last meals before executions called off is set free after conviction overturned
results.description: A DEATH row inmate is being set free after spending nearly 30 years behind bars and eating three last meals. The alleged killer's release comes after the US Supreme Court threw out the conviction last year and made shocking claims about the case. Richard Glossip was set free on Thursday after paying the $500,00 bail...

results.title: Four soldiers injured as troops survive IED blast, ambush in Borno
results.description: Four soldiers were injured on Saturday after troops on patrol survived an improvised explosive device (IED) blast and a subsequent ambush along the Damboa–Kumala–Maiduguri supply route in Borno State. The incident was disclosed by security expert Zagazola Makama in a post on his X handle on Sunday. results.title: Selena Gomez Teams Up with Cate Blanchett and Michael Fassbender for New Film
results.description: Selena Gomez is gearing up for her next film project, an untitled work directed by Brady Corbet, featuring Cate Blanchett and Michael Fassbender. This film, which is rumored to be rated X, follows Gomez's role in Emilia Perez. At the Cannes Film Festival, Blanchett confirmed her participation, while details about the film remain largely under wraps. The project promises to be ambitious, with a script longer than Corbet's previous film, The Brutalist. As the stars prepare for their upcoming roles, fans are eager to see what this collaboration will bring.

results.title: Death Valley review – Timothy Spall is having a total blast, and so will you!
results.description: The second series of this cheery Welsh cosy crime is as gentle as a pillow – and if it feels dangerously close to a spoof, that’s all part of the fun What a curious confection Death Valley is. It’s as cosy as crime drama gets and yet, thanks to its show-within-a-show concept, it manages to be oddly arch and knowing, too. Its body count challenges that of The Sopranos, but in its soothing episodic rhythms and Welsh valley quaintness, it remains as comfortable and predictable as Christmas. As we return, everything and nothing has changed. Janie Mallowan (Gwyneth Keyworth) has been promoted to detective inspector and is wondering if her seniority means she can no longer call herself “J-Dog”. She remains charming but also goofy and tactless; at one point, she complains about a teabag being left in a cuppa made for her by the wife of a recent murder victim. Continue reading...
